sections.Description:
The jellyfish-like Psions act as support units, providing passive bonuses to theVek. The Blast Psion is distinguished by its light grey coloration and bright orange cranial nodes. This Psion's presence causes other Vek to explode upon death, damaging adjacent tiles. The ability granted to the other Vek is calledExplosive Decay.
The Blast Psion can never spawn on missions withexplosive mines, even if they are the Psion for theArchive, Inc.island.
sections.Strategy:
The Blast Psion can complicate the resolution of any enemy on the map, as any enemy that dies will explode and deal collateral damage. If the Blast Psion can't be immediately taken out, look for weapons that can nullify other targets by either cancelling their attacks or pushing them away from buildings. It is possible to use the explosive decay effect to the players advantage to damage other Vek, provided caution is exercised in preventing collateral damage to the grid.
  - Vek will not explode if Blast Psion also dies as part of thesameattack resolution. E.g. if you push a regularLeaperinto a 1HP Blast Psion, the attack will kill them both and the Leaper won't explode. However, in sequenced deaths where Blast Psion dies as part of series of other deaths, explosions will continue to happen until Blast Psion dies as part of aseparateattack resolution.[1]
  - Vek explosions triggered by emerging Vek are processed after all Vek have finished emerging from underground. This may lead to unplanned outcomes when an explosive Vek dies due to blocking aspawn pointlocated adjacent to another spawn point. In this case, the Vek who emerges from the adjacent spawn point will be damaged by the explosion.[2]
  - If you kill a Vek with Explosive Decay by pushing it into a pit, the explosion will still damage adjacent tiles.
  - Beware, armored plate does NOT nullify the damage from the explosion.

: Flying units can move over any terrain tile.
Blast Psion
is one of the
Vek
from
Into The Breach
.
Description
[
]
The jellyfish-like Psions act as support units, providing passive bonuses to the
Vek
. The Blast Psion is distinguished by its light grey coloration and bright orange cranial nodes. This Psion's presence causes other Vek to explode upon death, damaging adjacent tiles. The ability granted to the other Vek is called
Explosive Decay
.
The Blast Psion can never spawn on missions with
explosive mines
, even if they are the Psion for the
Archive, Inc.
island.
Strategy
[
]
The Blast Psion can complicate the resolution of any enemy on the map, as any enemy that dies will explode and deal collateral damage. If the Blast Psion can't be immediately taken out, look for weapons that can nullify other targets by either cancelling their attacks or pushing them away from buildings. It is possible to use the explosive decay effect to the players advantage to damage other Vek, provided caution is exercised in preventing collateral damage to the grid.
Vek will not explode if Blast Psion also dies as part of the
same
attack resolution. E.g. if you push a regular
Leaper
into a 1HP Blast Psion, the attack will kill them both and the Leaper won't explode. However, in sequenced deaths where Blast Psion dies as part of series of other deaths, explosions will continue to happen until Blast Psion dies as part of a
separate
attack resolution.
[
1
]
Vek explosions triggered by emerging Vek are processed after all Vek have finished emerging from underground. This may lead to unplanned outcomes when an explosive Vek dies due to blocking a
spawn point
located adjacent to another spawn point. In this case, the Vek who emerges from the adjacent spawn point will be damaged by the explosion.
[
2
]
If you kill a Vek with Explosive Decay by pushing it into a pit, the explosion will still damage adjacent tiles.
Beware, armored plate does NOT nullify the damage from the explosion.
References

]

Blast Psion Resolution
, notice the final Firefly enemy not exploding as it dies, since it was after the Blast Psion's death is processed.
↑
